mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving sustained positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields a negative mean forward return or a Rank IC below 0.01 over any 24-month evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase signals strengthening corporate output and earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered observations during recovery phases, or the signal loses statistical significance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and wage growth, which directly supports corporate revenue expectations and yields positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal produces a negative mean forward return or fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.03 across consecutive 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation breaching the 3.0 percent annual threshold forces monetary authorities to tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of observations following the inflation threshold breach, or the signal predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production outpaces real GDP growth indicates supply-side efficiency gains and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields a negative mean forward return or a Rank IC below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during a late-cycle expansion signal weakening consumer demand and impending earnings revisions, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of triggered instances during late-cycle phases, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward validation.
